# 'gs branch submit --fill' uses the configured branch message
# generator to produce the PR title and body.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a new branch with a commit
git add feature.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature1' feature1

# configure the message generator
git config spice.messageGenerator 'printf "Generated Title\n\nGenerated body text"'

# submit with --fill; generator should produce the title/body
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

shamhub dump changes
cmpenvJSON stdout $WORK/golden/changes.json

-- repo/feature.txt --
Contents of feature

-- golden/changes.json --
[
  {
    "number": 1,
    "state": "open",
    "title": "Generated Title",
    "body": "Generated body text",
    "html_url": "$SHAMHUB_URL/alice/example/change/1",
    "head": {
      "repository": {
        "owner": "alice",
        "name": "example"
      },
      "ref": "feature1",
      "sha": "21e228ab8f98cad1f2427470f747c7b83c9b395c"
    },
    "base": {
      "repository": {
        "owner": "alice",
        "name": "example"
      },
      "ref": "main",
      "sha": "ece8ed7bb81d74cb6787309fa41b7deb2e0558a3"
    }
  }
]
